description: Candidate poor-cli tasks for comparing Claude-only, Codex-only, and poor-cli orchestrated runs.

tasks.title: Move v5 to legacy and create clean src package
tasks.prompt: Implement the v6 bootstrap package layout while preserving legacy code and keeping poor-cli --version at 6.0.0a1.

tasks.title: Implement SQLite RunStore, CAS, and append-only events
tasks.prompt: Persist runs, tasks, agents, artifacts, and events with SQLite plus content-addressed blobs.

tasks.title: Define core orchestration models
tasks.prompt: Define small serializable models for runs, plans, tasks, agents, budgets, artifacts, and context packets.

tasks.title: Implement v6 CLI skeleton
tasks.prompt: Expose plan, run, runs, inspect, and replay commands backed by RunStore.

tasks.title: Detect Claude, Codex, and generic shell agents
tasks.prompt: Detect local generic, Claude, and Codex agents and expose agents, agents doctor, and agents inspect.
success_criteria: missing agents do not break generic operation

tasks.title: Add LLM-required structured planner
tasks.prompt: Generate a persisted JSON task graph using a configured planner command or local Claude/Codex.
success_criteria: planner prompt and response are artifacts

tasks.title: Run confirmed tasks through agent adapters
tasks.prompt: Execute planned tasks sequentially after confirmation and record agent inputs, outputs, failures, and result artifacts.

tasks.title: Create inspectable context and handoff packets
tasks.prompt: Store bounded context packets before each task and handoff packets after completion or failure.
success_criteria: each executed task has context artifact

tasks.title: Reconstruct orchestration state from event history
tasks.prompt: Implement replay that rebuilds run and task state from persisted events without planner or agent calls.

tasks.title: Add confirmation, budget placeholders, and failure events
tasks.prompt: Add confirmation gating, inspectable budgets, and structured planner/agent/command failure events.
success_criteria: non-interactive run without --yes fails before execution
